headline: Oil tankers are blocked and threatened by Iranian mines in Strait of Hormuz
description: Several ships came under attack in the strait on Wednesday and Thursday. Tehran has been blocking maritime traffic in the area since the war launched by Israel and the US began.

headline: Benjamin Netanyahu, a prime minister without checks and balances
description: The strikes in Iran have further reduced the political space for opposition in Israel, even as concerns mount over a possible ground offensive in Lebanon. Today, only Donald Trump seems capable of influencing the Israeli leader.

headline: How Paris mobility transformed under Anne Hidalgo's two terms
description: Between 2014 and 2026, the streets of Paris underwent sweeping changes, notably with the pedestrianization of the riverbanks and the creation of numerous bike lanes. Pollution levels in the French capital have dropped, but as this improvement has been seen across the region and throughout France, the question of causality remains unresolved.

headline: Some cancers may be less common among vegetarians
description: A study based on 15 years of monitoring several cohorts in four countries found that individuals who abstain from eating meat have a lower risk of developing multiple myeloma, pancreatic, breast, prostate and kidney cancer.

headline: Jeffrey Epstein's Parisian lives
description: Over 18 years, the extremely kitsch property at 22 Avenue Foch served Epstein as a European office, a salon for artists and scientists and, above all, one of the places where he committed his sexual crimes.

'The Islamism of the Iranian regime is now reduced to a rather worn veneer'
description: The appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as supreme leader, in a regime that calls itself an Islamic Republic, already signals a form of defeat in the ongoing war waged by the US and Israel, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

headline: Pete Hegseth, the embodiment of Trump's war in Iran
description: The US secretary of defense, a former Fox News host, has ardently defended the operation against the Islamic Republic. He has disregarded rules of engagement and boasted about American supremacy.

headline: One week before France's local elections, electorate leans right, poll shows
description: With environmental issues taking a back seat, security issues coming to the fore and a clear increase in support for the far-right Rassemblement National, the survey conducted by Ipsos BVA for Le Monde, Cevipof and the Jean Jaurès Foundation paints an unprecedented picture of the French political landscape.
